findings.description: Comment cross-references the WRONG /dev standard number. Text reads '/dev Standard 6.spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 2 P3.6.' — but Standard 6 in agents/style-inspector.md is English-Only; the venv co-location rule is Standard 3 (Use Source venv, Not python3). Additionally the period between '6' and 'spec' looks like a typo (should be '/' or whitespace). Recommend: '# same line as python3 per /dev Standard 3 (use source venv) — spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 2 P3.6.'

non_findings_with_rationale.rationale: All CJK characters are bound user-facing prompt-output strings: rank labels (见习学徒/初级工匠/熟练工匠/资深工匠/宗师级), neutral-header fallback text (line 40-41, 72-73), user-rating tail phrase (lines 96-101). Explicitly covered by the Cycle-2 exemption at agents/style-inspector.md:322 classes (i), (ii), and (iv). Non-weakening clause preserved: code comments above the heredoc remain English.

RANK_BOUNDARIES table entries (lines 101-106), rank_for_score fallback (line 113), and agent-init fallback (line 149) all emit one of the five canonical rank labels — bound to the spec-20260518-225715 §5.1 role per exemption class (i). Stderr/diagnostic strings remain English (lines 125-128, 132-136), consistent with the non-weakening clause.

All CJK occurrences are PRE-EXISTING (not in this cycle's diff). Lines 253 and 1486-1488 are wrapped in <USER_VERBATIM> tags (verbatim user-binding-quote exemption at agents/style-inspector.md:320). Lines 357/609/661/801 are verbatim spec-attributed quotes ('per spec 5.1 line 113: ...', 'per spec-20260518-225715 §5.2 line 167: ...'). The cycle's diff to commands/dev.md introduced ZERO new CJK characters.

File is under docs/ — Standard 6 scope-exclusion zone ('Do NOT scan docs/ -- documentation and planning files may legitimately contain non-English content'). Spec files in docs/dev/specs/ are explicitly out-of-scope for Standard 6 enforcement.

The literal 5000 is a deliberate test-input size chosen to exceed any plausible cap value; the comment at lines 109-110 explicitly documents the choice ('Use 5000 lines to exceed any plausible cap and assert the guard fires'). Per Standard 2 guidance, 'Constants that genuinely never change' are acceptable. The script also accepts --hooks-dir parameter so the only test variable is parameterized.

The EVENT_DELTAS dict mirrors the canonical spec-5.1 score-event table verbatim; these are spec-contract constants, NOT environment-variable values. Per Standard 2 'Constants that genuinely never change' rule. The script's mutable inputs (--agent, --event, --note, --scores-file) ARE all parameterized.

Single dense paragraph; however, it enumerates four discrete exemption classes with spec attribution, scope binding, and a re-affirmation of the non-weakening clause. The density is functional — every clause encodes an enforcement boundary; bullet-splitting would scatter the binding without reducing the byte count. Recommend keeping as-is; no rewrite warranted.

These lines REFERENCE the historical decimal labels (Sub-step 12.0 / 12.1) in retrospective prose ('formerly labelled with a decimal sub-step (eliminated per ... Standard 4)') to document the integer-step-numbering migration. The actual step structure no longer uses decimals — the active headings on lines 824 and 865 are 'Apply spec section updates' and 'Apply Mascot score-update events'. Retrospective references to deprecated numbering are documentation of compliance, not violations.

5-line comment explaining the test-writer enrolment is functional traceability (spec attribution + cross-file consistency rationale). Standard 8 permits 'Concise technical explanations (≤3 lines) providing context'; this is 4 lines of functional content (spec id, agent name, cross-file pointers, failure mode). Acceptable.
3-line comment registering test-writer in CP_AGENTS with spec attribution. Functional and concise.

Both files add justification comments for the SKIP_NAMES additions (spec-attributed runtime-telemetry exclusion). tree.py also adds an 8-line block explaining the PATH-AWARE leak suppression — the verbosity is justified by the NEGATIVE control AC-09 cited in the comment (a naïve i.name == 'specs' check would over-suppress). Functional documentation of an invariant.
